description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted in a green and gray camouflage pattern, suitable for blending into urban or forested environments. The helicopter features a long, slender tail boom ending in a tail rotor, and a main rotor with four blades, each tipped with yellow markings. The cockpit is enclosed with large, angular windows that provide wide visibility for the pilots, and the body includes side doors and small round windows. The landing gear consists of two robust metal skids, reinforced with crossbars for stability during landing. The overall design suggests a light utility helicopter, likely used for reconnaissance, transport, or patrol missions in urban settings.
description: A camouflaged military-style helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and sturdy metal landing skids.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the cockpit windows, main rotor, and the full length of the landing skids.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail boom, tail rotor, and the camouflage pattern along the fuselage.
description_view_2: Left side view displaying the side doors, round window, and the alignment of the main and tail rotors.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the cockpit glazing, rotor hub, and the right-side landing skid.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, each with a dark color and yellow tips, extending symmetrically from the rotor hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: A two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, with red and white markings on the blade tips.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel metal landing skids, painted black, with crossbars for reinforcement and a simple, utilitarian design.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Three large, angular cockpit windows at the front and sides, slightly tinted, with visible framing and no apparent markings.
